source_metadata.title_raw: Thomas E. Montgomery - A-18-184(a) - December 24, 2018 - San Diego

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
( 916) 322-5660 • Fax ( 916) 322-0886 
Thomas E. Montgomery 
County Counsel 
Office of County Counsel 
1600 Pacific Highway, Room 355 
San Diego, CA 92101 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-184(a) 
Dear Mr. Montgomery: 
December 24, 2018 
This letter supplements our previously issued Montgomery Advice Letter, No. A-18-184, 
regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). In regard to 
your question and facts outlined in your previous request, you have asked us to clarify our statement 
that decisions involving the LHR project and decisions associated with the general plan amendment 
necessary for several projects including the LHR project can be segmented "[t]o the extent that the 
County makes a final decision on the LHR project and a final decision that the general plan will be 
amended to encompass the LHR project prior to, and regardless of, the further deliberations on the 
general plan." Additionally, you seek guidance regarding the application of Regulation 18706(c), 
which permits an official to "participate in the final vote to adopt, reject, or amend the general plan" 
once "separate decisions related to a budget or general plan affecting the entire jurisdiction have 
been finalized." 
Based upon the facts previously provided, you have indicated that the general plan 
amendment is necessary for several projects including the LHR project and that the County 
contemplates only one general plan amendment approval vote, which would encompass all changes 
to the general plan from the several projects. To adequately segment the decision under Regulation 
18706, the board of supervisors must first discuss the LHR Project and, except for the final yes or 
no vote regarding the general plan, make all decisions specific to the project without any 
involvement or participation by Supervisor Hom. Only after the decisions regarding the LHR 
Project are made may Supervisor Hom take part in other decisions related to the general plan 
amendment. Subsequent to the initial decisions regarding the LHR project, no additional 
discussions or decisions regarding the project may be conducted or made other than the final yes or 
no vote regarding the general plan amendment. Under Regulation 18706(c), Supervisor Hom may 
take part in the final yes or no vote regarding the general plan amendment, but again, there may be 
no further discussions of the LHR Project. 
If the LHR Project is initially approved but the general plan amendment fails, the board of 
supervisors may reopen discussions, including discussions of the LHR Project. As stated above, 
however, the board must first discuss the LHR project and make decisions regarding the project 
without any involvement or participation by Supervisor Hom. Supervisor Hom may then take part 


File No. A-18-184(a) 
Page No. 2 
in other decisions related to the general plan amendment, as well as the final yes or no vote 
regarding the general plan amendment, so long as there are no further discussions of the LHR 
Project. 
JW:jgl 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
ac 
Seni r Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor seeks clarification on how to properly segment decisions involving the LHR project from broader general plan amendment decisions under Regulation 18706(c), which allows an official to participate in final votes on general plan amendments once separate decisions have been finalized.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that Supervisor Hom must recuse from all LHR project-specific discussions and decisions except the final general plan amendment vote. The board must complete all LHR project deliberations first, then Supervisor Hom may participate in other general plan matters and the final amendment vote, with no further LHR project discussions permitted.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is a supplemental advice letter (A-18-184(a)) clarifying a prior advice letter (A-18-184). The document contains both a question section and detailed conclusion addressing conflict of interest segmentation rules. High confidence in extraction.; duplicate_note: Amendment PDF identical to parent — FPPC replaced original

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You seek guidance regarding the application of Regulation 18706(c), which permits an official to 'participate in the final vote to adopt, reject, or amend the general plan' once 'separate decisions related to a budget or general plan affecting the entire jurisdiction have been finalized.' Specifically, you have asked us to clarify our statement that decisions involving the LHR project and decisions associated with the general plan amendment necessary for several projects including the LHR project can be segmented '[t]o the extent that the County makes a final decision on the LHR project and a final decision that the general plan will be amended to encompass the LHR project prior to, and regardless of, the further deliberations on the general plan.'

CONCLUSION: To adequately segment the decision under Regulation 18706, the board of supervisors must first discuss the LHR Project and, except for the final yes or no vote regarding the general plan, make all decisions specific to the project without any involvement or participation by Supervisor Hom. Only after the decisions regarding the LHR Project are made may Supervisor Hom take part in other decisions related to the general plan amendment. Subsequent to the initial decisions regarding the LHR project, no additional discussions or decisions regarding the project may be conducted or made other than the final yes or no vote regarding the general plan amendment. Under Regulation 18706(c), Supervisor Hom may take part in the final yes or no vote regarding the general plan amendment, but again, there may be no further discussions of the LHR Project.

embedding.summary: This supplemental advice letter clarifies how to properly segment conflict of interest decisions involving the LHR project from broader general plan amendment decisions under California Political Reform Act Regulation 18706(c). The FPPC provides detailed procedural guidance on when Supervisor Hom can and cannot participate in various phases of decision-making.
